title: Dogfood evidence + Batch Gemini review + CHANGELOG
description: Wave 4 closure. Three artifacts: (A) dogfood-evidence.md — run `fab doctor` against pcf itself (fabric_language='zh-CN') and capture the Chinese output as evidence the feature works end-to-end; also capture the en-locale output for comparison. (B) code-review.md — run batch Gemini review over the rc.26 diff (per memory feedback_review_batching: ONCE at end, not per-task) and capture verdict + any follow-ups. (C) CHANGELOG.md — add v2.0.0-rc.26 (Unreleased) section summarizing user-facing impact (`fab doctor` now respects fabric_language). NO version bump in this task — that's the /release-rc skill's responsibility after this RC is approved.

files.change: Document the dogfood verification. Three sections: (1) Setup — confirm pcf .fabric/fabric-config.json has fabric_language='zh-CN'; (2) Run — paste literal output of `fab doctor` (current Chinese), comparing against the pre-rc.26 English output captured at session start in this very conversation; (3) Comparison table — for ~5 representative checks (bootstrap_anchor, knowledge_relevance_paths_dangling, serve_lock, onboard_coverage, cite_coverage), show side-by-side English vs Chinese rendering of name/message/remediation. Should also include en-locale screenshot proof: temporarily set fabric_language='en', run, capture, restore. ~60-100 LoC of markdown.

Batch Gemini review of the rc.26 diff. Invoke via: `maestro delegate "PURPOSE: Code review for rc.26 doctor i18n closure (commits TASK-01..TASK-05). Verify: locale resolution correctness, translation key naming consistency, plural handling, protected_tokens preservation in zh-CN, snapshot test robustness, no regression on existing doctor tests. TASK: Read planning-context.md + plan.json + all 6 TASK files + final diff. Report severity-ranked findings. MODE: analysis. CONTEXT: @.workflow/.lite-plan/rc26-doctor-i18n-closure-2026-05-19/** @packages/server/src/services/doctor.ts @packages/shared/src/i18n/**. EXPECTED: severity-ranked finding list with file:line refs. CONSTRAINTS: focus on i18n correctness + maintainability; ignore stylistic preferences." --role review --mode analysis --rule analysis-review-code-quality`. Paste delegate output into this file. Note: blocking-severity findings must be addressed before RC tag; warnings can be deferred to next RC. Capture user disposition on each finding.

files.target: top of file, above v2.0.0-rc.25 entry
files.change:
Insert new section:

## v2.0.0-rc.26 (Unreleased)

**Theme**: doctor i18n closure

### Added
- `resolveFabricLocale(projectRoot)` helper in `@fenglimg/fabric-shared` reads `.fabric/fabric-config.json::fabric_language` as authoritative locale source (closes KT-DEC-9004 contract gap)
- 2-locale snapshot test ensures `fab doctor` output stays bilingually correct

### Changed
- `fab doctor` now respects `fabric_language` config (previously hardcoded English regardless of config). 84 check messages translated. zh-CN renders Chinese narrative with `relevance_paths`/`stable_id`/`narrow-scope`/`pending`/`canonical` preserved verbatim (zh-CN-hybrid style).
- `packages/server/src/services/serve-lock.ts` and `packages/cli/src/commands/doctor.ts` consume `resolveFabricLocale` instead of `detectNodeLocale`

### Fixed
- Long-standing zh-CN user pain (rc.12 grill-me follow-up): `fab doctor` output now matches user-configured language.

### Notes
- Other CLI commands (`install`, `config`, `serve`, `uninstall`) continue to use env-locale (`detectNodeLocale`); migration deferred to future RC.
- Machine-readable `code` field on each check (e.g. `knowledge_relevance_paths_dangling`) unchanged — contract preserved.

implementation: Verify TASK-05 is green: `pnpm typecheck && pnpm lint && pnpm test` all pass.
Confirm pcf .fabric/fabric-config.json::fabric_language is 'zh-CN' (set earlier in this session) — if not, set it.
Build cli locally: `pnpm --filter @fenglimg/fabric-cli build` (or whatever existing build command is used; check package.json).
Run `fab doctor` against pcf and capture full output (use bash redirection to a tmp file or paste-into-md inline).
Temporarily change fabric_language to 'en', run `fab doctor` again, capture. Restore to 'zh-CN' immediately. Document the restore step in dogfood-evidence.md so it's reproducible.
Author dogfood-evidence.md with both outputs + comparison table. ~5 representative checks side-by-side.
Invoke `maestro delegate` with the review prompt (see files[1].change for full prompt). Capture the response to code-review.md.

If review surfaces blocking findings: address them inline (small fix commit `fix(rc26): <description> (review remediation)`), update code-review.md disposition section. If only warnings: note disposition (`accepted as-is` / `deferred to rc.27` etc.) and move on.
Final verification: `pnpm typecheck && pnpm lint && pnpm test` all green.
Commit: `chore(rc26): dogfood evidence + code review + CHANGELOG (TASK-06)`
Inform user: RC is ready for release. Suggest invoking `/release-rc` skill to bump version to v2.0.0-rc.26 + push tag.

reference.pattern: rc.25 TASK-13 (Gemini batch review), rc.24 TASK-12 (CHANGELOG + dogfood) — identical closure structure.
reference.examples: rc.25 TASK-12 (CHANGELOG) + rc.25 TASK-13 (Gemini code-review) — closure pattern established in prior RCs.

rationale.chosen_approach: Single closure task bundling dogfood + review + CHANGELOG. Per memory feedback_review_batching: ONE Gemini review at end, not per-task. This task delivers the closure narrative.
alternatives_considered: Split CHANGELOG into its own task (TASK-06a) — rejected: CHANGELOG is a one-line addition, no need for separate atomic commit
Embed dogfood evidence into code-review.md — rejected: separation of concerns; dogfood = empirical verification, code-review = adversarial assessment
decision_factors: Memory feedback_review_batching: batch review at end
rc.24/rc.25 closure precedent matches this structure
Per-task commit for the closure artifacts
rationale.tradeoffs: If Gemini review surfaces blocker, this task expands to include a remediation sub-commit — documented in implementation step 8 as acceptable contingency.

fab doctor with fabric_language='en' renders English (visual inspection of dogfood-evidence.md)
